description: Baseline semantic decode coverage benchmark over all captured matches using current decoder capabilities.

asi.hypothesis: Establish a stable baseline metric for semantic decode coverage before exploring new opcode/field decoders.
asi.baseline_observations: Game mode is decoded for all benchmarked matches, but no player hero assignments are recovered and no fully populated player records exist yet.
asi.bottleneck: Most decrypted traffic still lacks semantic labeling beyond roster, positions, and a few stat/property streams.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate high-frequency opcodes around roster/setup and scoreboard-related payloads to recover hero mapping and richer player-state fields.

description: Added heuristic opcode 1067 state decoding to surface death/respawn state transitions alongside existing damage spikes.

asi.hypothesis: Opcode 1067 contains player state transitions; treating idx=0 state=3/1 as death/respawn should surface additional semantic event categories.
asi.learning: The heuristic increased distinct event categories from 1 to 3 across the capture corpus, indicating opcode 1067 carries repeatable lifecycle information.
asi.confidence_note: This is still heuristic labeling, not a confirmed semantic mapping, but it is stable enough to be useful as a decoding lead.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate opcode 1011 and snapshot/roster cross-links to recover hero assignments or richer per-player state.

description: Treated opcode 1113 as an earlier snapshot variant, recovering roster records from short/pre-match captures that previously decoded as zero players.

asi.hypothesis: Opcode 1113 reuses the 1114-style roster record layout and can bootstrap player rows when the capture ends before normal 1114 snapshots appear.
asi.learning: Decoding 1113 with the snapshot parser recovered the missing six-player roster in the short HF_Ranked capture, increasing corpus player coverage from 48 to 54 rows.
asi.artifact: 1113 records use the same key offsets for handle/entity/team/UUID as 1114 in the short capture examined.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for other near-snapshot opcodes or per-player setup messages that can populate heroes or complete player records.

description: Decoded opcode 1011 into per-player spawn/setup fields, recovering stable hero-type IDs, spawn coordinates, and spawn health values for full-match captures.

asi.hypothesis: Opcode 1011 is a per-player spawn/setup blob; decoding stable fields from it should materially increase semantic coverage even before hero-name mapping is solved.
asi.learning: 1011 cleanly links to roster entries by entity ID and yields stable hero_type_id plus spawn position/health fields for 42 player rows across the corpus.
asi.artifact: Useful offsets: 0:4 hero_type_id candidate, 10:12 entity_id, 12:16 team, 16:24 spawn XY, 112:120 spawn health pair.
asi.next_action_hint: Cluster hero_type_id values across matches and correlate them with other setup opcodes or external hero stat signatures to map hero_type_id -> hero name.

description: Corrected opcode 1011 field alignment: spawn coordinates live at byte offsets 18/22 and spawn health at 138/142, improving decoded setup-field coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Several 1011 fields were misaligned by 2 bytes; correcting those offsets should turn placeholder setup fields into plausible coordinates and health values.
asi.learning: 1011 uses mixed alignment: entity/team fields are aligned, but key float fields are shifted by 2 bytes. The corrected offsets produce plausible spawn XY and health values.
asi.artifact: Confirmed useful 1011 offsets now: 0:4 hero_type_id, 8:12 entity_id word, 12:16 team, 18:22 spawn X, 22:26 spawn Y, 138:142 spawn health, 142:146 spawn health max.
asi.next_action_hint: Use corrected spawn stats plus hero_type_id clustering to fingerprint heroes, or attack 1052/1087 as likely sources for persistent hero stats and inventory-like state.

description: Decoded opcode 1052 as a typed per-entity scalar stream, capturing stable scalar stat slots for all full-match hero records.

asi.hypothesis: Opcode 1052 is not just noise; it is a typed scalar-stat stream keyed by entity ID, and preserving those typed values increases semantic state coverage.
asi.learning: 1052 reliably attaches to hero entities and exposes repeatable scalar slots (at least two per player in full matches), even if the exact semantics of each scalar type are still open.
asi.artifact: Current 1052 parse: entity_id at 2:4, scalar_value at 8:12, scalar_type at byte 12.
asi.next_action_hint: Name the common 1052 scalar types by correlating them with hero archetype, spawn health, movement, or known Vainglory stat ranges; then attack 1087 for inventory-like state.

description: Decoded opcode 1087 as a typed extended-property stream, preserving per-player u16 property codes and latest values/aux data for full-match hero records.

asi.hypothesis: Opcode 1087 is another structured per-entity state stream; retaining its typed property codes should expand player-state coverage beyond the simpler 1052/1086 channels.
asi.learning: 1087 reliably attaches to hero entities and yields a dense set of repeatable property codes per player, likely including inventory/stateful gameplay fields.
asi.artifact: Current 1087 parse: entity_id at 2:4, prop_code at 12:14, prop_value at 14:16, aux_value at 28:32.
asi.next_action_hint: Cluster frequent 1087 prop codes by hero_type_id and over time to separate persistent loadout fields from counters/items, then try naming the stable families.

description: Recovered hero-type and stable profile identifiers directly from snapshot records, extending hero-linked setup coverage to all 54 decoded player rows including short/incomplete captures.

asi.hypothesis: Snapshot records themselves carry hero-linked identifiers; if rec[16:18] mirrors hero_type_id and rec[20:24] is a stable per-player profile field, short captures can gain the same setup coverage as full matches.
asi.learning: rec[16:18] in 1113/1114 snapshots matches the 1011 hero_type_id candidate, and rec[20:24] is a stable nonzero profile identifier. This extends hero-type coverage from 42 to all 54 player rows.
asi.artifact: 1113/1114 snapshot fields now treated as: rec[16:18] hero_type_id, rec[20:24] snapshot_profile_id.
asi.next_action_hint: Use timeline diffs over snapshot profile/hero-type anchored players to infer level/item/ability counters, especially under ARAL starting-level constraints.

description: Decoded snapshot activity flags and surfaced first-activation timeline events, separating players who actually become active from roster entries in incomplete or never-started captures.

asi.hypothesis: Snapshot bytes 9/10 are activity/readiness flags; using them should distinguish roster placeholders from players who actually enter the timeline and improve handling of incomplete captures.
asi.learning: rec[9] and rec[10] transition from 0 to 1 when a player becomes active in 1113/1114 snapshots. This yields meaningful 'became active' timeline events and explains incomplete-match edge cases.
asi.artifact: Snapshot active flag heuristic: active when rec[9] == 1 and rec[10] == 1.
asi.next_action_hint: Use active-player anchored timelines to search for level/item/ability counters whose deltas start only after activation, especially in ARAL captures with level-4 starts.

description: Generalized opcode 1086 into a typed counter/property map while preserving the existing gold/xp aliases, expanding timeline state coverage for full-match hero records.

asi.hypothesis: Opcode 1086 carries more than just the two known gold/xp channels; retaining all typed counters should expose additional progression signals for later naming.
asi.learning: 1086 is a broader typed counter stream. The known 0x3e/0x4d aliases remain useful, but storing all property types gives a denser per-player progression timeline for full matches.
asi.artifact: Player.prop_counters now captures latest counter by opcode-1086 property type, alongside existing gold_counter/xp_counter aliases.
asi.next_action_hint: Correlate 1086 type families with activation time, ARAL start-level expectations, and 1052/1087 changes to isolate level, XP, and inventory-adjacent fields.

description: Rebased the benchmark after re-validating assumptions: reward typed timeline coverage and structural player completeness, not overconfident gold/xp or unstable 1087 sequence fields.

asi.hypothesis: The benchmark had started rewarding potentially incorrect semantic assumptions; resetting it around typed timeline coverage should better match the real decoding goal.
asi.learning: 1087 needed a safer family/target interpretation and 1086 gold/xp aliases remain tentative. Structural completeness plus typed timeline coverage is a more trustworthy optimization target for now.
asi.next_action_hint: Probe post-death counter pulses and other timeline-correlated updates to separate kill rewards, item effects, and ability/cooldown-related fields under the corrected baseline.

description: Added post-death counter-pulse detection, surfacing a new reward-like timeline category when multiple players receive synchronized opcode-1086 updates right after a death.

asi.hypothesis: Kills/deaths should cause correlated counter changes; scanning immediately after death states for synchronized 1086 updates can surface reward windows before exact gold/xp attribution is solved.
asi.learning: Several matches show repeatable post-death counter pulses, most often in opcode-1086 family types like 0x40/0x3e/0x4d, indicating kill-adjacent reward/state updates are present and detectable.
asi.artifact: New helper detect_post_death_counter_pulses() emits neutral reward-like events without overclaiming exact semantics.
asi.next_action_hint: Differentiate which post-death pulse families represent killer gold, team XP, bounty, or respawn-side state by comparing affected player sets and delta magnitudes.

description: Split opcode-1087 state into self-targeted extended properties versus off-target interaction properties, exposing a clearer separation between local state and combat/objective-like interactions.

asi.hypothesis: 1087 mixes self-state and target-directed updates; separating self-targeted versus off-target messages should better align the stream with items/cooldowns versus combat/minion interactions.
asi.learning: A substantial subset of players show off-target 1087 interactions once self-target noise is removed, which is promising for combat, minion, or objective semantics.
asi.artifact: Player.interaction_props now holds 1087 entries where target_id != source entity, while self-targeted entries stay in Player.extended_props.
asi.next_action_hint: Cluster interaction_props by target class (hero vs non-hero like 2000/2008) and family byte to separate combat, minion last-hit, and item/talent side effects.

description: Added first-seen minion-like interaction events for opcode-1087 target-directed updates into the 2000-2099 entity range, surfacing a new farm/objective-adjacent timeline category.

asi.hypothesis: Off-target 1087 updates into the 2000-2099 entity range are likely lane/minion interactions; surfacing them as tentative timeline events should expose farm/objective-adjacent structure without overcommitting exact semantics.
asi.learning: The 2000-2099 target band is common enough to form a distinct interaction class, and exposing it adds a stable new event category across the corpus.
asi.artifact: New detect_interaction_timeline_events() helper emits tentative 'minion-like interaction' events from 1087 non-self target updates.
asi.next_action_hint: Break the 2000-2099 target band down further and compare counter deltas around these minion-like events to isolate last-hit gold/xp versus generic lane-state updates.

description: Added first-seen hero-target 1087 interaction events as tentative ability-like combat signals, giving the timeline a distinct cast/combat-adjacent category.

asi.hypothesis: Off-target 1087 updates aimed at other hero entities are likely combat or ability-side interactions; surfacing them as tentative hero-target events should expose another meaningful timeline category.
asi.learning: Hero-target 1087 interactions are common enough to form a stable separate event class from minion-like interactions, which supports the idea that 1087 mixes combat-side signaling with local state.
asi.artifact: detect_interaction_timeline_events() now emits 'ability-like hero interaction' events for first-seen hero-target 1087 updates and 'minion-like interaction' events for 2000-2099 targets.
asi.next_action_hint: Correlate hero-target interaction families with nearby 1053 damage pulses and state changes to distinguish ability casts from generic attacks or status effects.

description: Correlated hero-target 1087 interactions with nearby target death states to surface tentative kill-like combat events in the match timeline.

asi.hypothesis: If 1087 hero-target interactions are combat-side signals, some should cluster immediately before target death states and can be surfaced as kill-like windows.
asi.learning: A subset of hero-target 1087 interactions consistently precede target death states within ~1s, which is strong evidence that this stream carries combat-resolution information rather than only passive state.
asi.artifact: New detect_kill_like_interactions() helper emits tentative kill-like events when hero-target 1087 updates are followed by a target death transition.
asi.next_action_hint: Use kill-like windows to compare which 1086/1052 families change on killer, victim, and teammates, then isolate actual gold/xp/bounty counters and maybe assist signals.

description: Added assist-like death-window detection by looking for multiple distinct hero-target 1087 interaction sources before a target dies.

asi.hypothesis: If multiple heroes emit target-directed 1087 interactions into the same victim shortly before a death, that window is a good assist-like candidate even before exact scoreboard fields are found.
asi.learning: Multi-attacker death windows do exist in the corpus and can be surfaced separately from single-source kill-like windows, giving the timeline a tentative assist category.
asi.artifact: New detect_assist_like_interactions() helper emits assist-like events when 2+ distinct sources target the same victim within 1s before death.
asi.next_action_hint: Use assist-like and kill-like windows to identify which 1086/1052 families increase on the killer only versus all nearby attackers, then promote the most consistent ones into tentative bounty/xp/item-side annotations.

description: Inferred ARAL talent-like offer and choice IDs from early self-targeted opcode-1087 family 0xbc bursts, adding per-player loadout-style state and a new timeline category.

asi.hypothesis: Early self-targeted 1087 family 0xbc bursts encode ARAL talent/loadout setup; the initial high-valued entries look like offered options and the later distinct value looks like the selected choice.
asi.learning: This heuristic recovers talent-like choice IDs for 41 players and fits the ARAL-only context well. Some players show 1-3 early offer IDs followed by a later distinct chosen ID, matching the game's talent selection flow.
asi.artifact: Player.talent_offer_ids and Player.talent_choice_id now derived from self-target 1087 family 0xbc sequences, gated to ARAL modes.
asi.next_action_hint: Correlate talent_choice_id clusters with later scalar/stat families and hero-target interaction families to identify talent-induced cooldown/stat modifications versus base hero behavior.

description: Tracked per-player hero-target and minion-target 1087 interaction families, turning tentative combat-cast and farm participation into reusable player-state fields.

asi.hypothesis: Once 1087 interactions are split by target class, the source player should expose persistent ability-like and farm-like participation families that are useful beyond one-off events.
asi.learning: Hero-target and 2000-2099-target interaction families are stable enough to retain directly on the player model, giving reusable ability-participation and farm-participation signals.
asi.artifact: Player.hero_interaction_families and Player.minion_interaction_families now accumulate 1087 family bytes by target class; evaluator tracks ability_players and farm_players.
asi.next_action_hint: Use talent_choice_id × hero_interaction_families × scalar/prop families to isolate talent-modified abilities, cooldown-related self-state, or farm reward channels.

description: Inferred recurring post-minion opcode-1086 reward families per player, turning minion-target interactions into reusable farm-reward channels.

asi.hypothesis: If minion-target 1087 interactions really represent farm/last-hit activity, the same source player should repeatedly receive a small set of 1086 counter-family updates immediately afterward.
asi.learning: This pattern is strong enough to recover stable farm-reward channel types per player. In multiple matches the same 1086 families recur after 2000-2099 interaction windows, which supports the farm-reward interpretation.
asi.artifact: New detect_farm_reward_channels() helper populates Player.farm_reward_types from recurring post-minion 1086 pulses.
asi.next_action_hint: Differentiate which farm_reward_types are likely gold versus XP by comparing co-occurrence with existing 0x3e/0x4d aliases and by checking whether one family appears without the other on shared-lane interactions.

description: Recovered recurring self-state followup channels after hero-target interactions, adding tentative cooldown-like 1052/1087 channels for players with repeatable post-cast self updates.

asi.hypothesis: Real ability activations should be followed by recurring self-state updates on the source player; repeated post-interaction 1052 or self-target 1087 channels are good cooldown-like candidates.
asi.learning: A small but real subset of players shows repeatable post-cast self channels, strong enough to retain as tentative cooldown/ability-side state rather than one-off noise.
asi.artifact: Player.ability_followup_scalar_types and Player.ability_followup_prop_families now capture recurring post-hero-interaction self channels; cooldown-like events are emitted once per player.
asi.next_action_hint: Cross-tab cooldown-like channels against talent_choice_id and hero_interaction_families to split base ability patterns from talent-modified ones, then revisit hero identity mapping using these richer fingerprints.

description: Added per-event xp-like and gold-like gain attribution around minion and death windows using recurring 1086 0x3e/0x4d counter deltas.

asi.hypothesis: The two best-behaved 1086 counter families can be attributed more concretely by looking at their deltas inside farm and death reward windows, yielding replay-usable xp-like and gold-like event annotations.
asi.learning: 0x3e and 0x4d now behave well enough to drive per-event xp-like and gold-like gain annotations around minion windows and kill/assist windows without requiring exact final semantics yet.
asi.artifact: New detect_resource_gain_events() helper emits farm xp-like/gold-like gains and kill-window / assist-window xp-like/gold-like gains from 1086 delta tracking.
asi.next_action_hint: Use the emitted xp-like gain stream plus base-stat jump clusters to infer level-up thresholds or at least level-like milestone events, then wire the richer timeline into the dashboard API path.

description: Tracked per-player xp-like and gold-like gain evidence from farm and kill windows, turning resource attribution into reusable player-state for the replay timeline.

asi.hypothesis: If replay-grade attribution is the goal, resource gain evidence should live both as timeline events and as reusable per-player state, not only as raw events.
asi.learning: Farm-driven xp-like and gold-like attribution is strong enough to promote to player-level state. Kill-window gain evidence exists but is still sparse, so kill-side attribution remains weaker than farm-side attribution.
asi.artifact: Player.xp_like_gain_events, Player.gold_like_gain_events, Player.kill_window_gain_events, and Player.assist_window_gain_events now accumulate from detect_resource_gain_events().
asi.next_action_hint: Use xp_like_gain_events together with activation order and repeated counter milestones to infer level-like progression events, then propagate the enriched timeline into vg_dashboard_server.py so the browser dashboard can replay it.

description: Added level-like milestone detection by combining xp-like counter updates with clustered self-stat jumps, yielding approximate progression events for replay timelines.

asi.hypothesis: Even without an explicit decoded level field, xp-like counter increases combined with clustered self-stat jumps should reveal approximate level-up milestones that are useful for replay and further reverse engineering.
asi.learning: This heuristic recovers level-like milestones for a meaningful subset of players, especially in longer ARAL captures, and matches the intuition that progression should couple xp-like changes with base-stat shifts.
asi.artifact: Player.level_like_events and Player.estimated_level_like now advance from detect_level_like_milestones(); events are emitted as 'level-like milestone -> approx level N'.
asi.next_action_hint: Propagate the enriched event timeline and player state into vg_dashboard_server.py / dashboard JSON, then keep refining exact level / kill-reward / item semantics against these replay-friendly annotations.

description: Added conservative item-like loadout detection from later self-targeted 1087 family 0xbc changes, surfacing a first replay-friendly purchase/equip-style category.

asi.hypothesis: Later self-targeted 1087 family 0xbc values that are distinct from the early talent sequence likely represent item/loadout changes rather than talent offers, and are useful for replay even before exact item names are mapped.
asi.learning: A small but nonzero subset of players shows later unique 0xbc self-values after the early talent phase, enough to justify a conservative item-like category. Coverage is low, so this remains tentative.
asi.artifact: Player.item_like_ids now accumulates later self-targeted 1087 family 0xbc values outside the constant/talent set; events are emitted as 'item-like loadout id N'.
asi.next_action_hint: Cross-check these item-like ids against subsequent stat/cooldown changes to separate real item purchases from other loadout mutations, then start mirroring the enriched timeline through vg_dashboard_server.py for the browser replay.

description: Rebased autoresearch onto full-match scoreboard replay state, filtered resource totals to plausible raw 1086 counters, and surfaced gameplay-focused dashboard fields for level, kills, deaths, CS, gold, and XP.

asi.hypothesis: The session had drifted toward generic event labels; resetting the benchmark around replay-usable scoreboard state and treating 1086 0x3e/0x4d as plausible raw totals should create a better baseline and immediately fix the obviously wrong dashboard gold/xp display.
asi.learning: The new scoreboard-focused baseline scores 7 full gameplay captures / 42 active players. Raw 1086 totals now produce plausible gold/xp magnitudes in the CLI dashboard, while kill attribution remains sparse and is the biggest remaining gameplay gap.
asi.artifact: New primary metric scoreboard_score=5504 with secondary metrics centered on gold/xp timelines, death coverage, kills, CS, level-ups, and replay-ready players.
asi.next_action_hint: Improve kill attribution and level/CS confidence by correlating death windows and stat-jump windows with additional 1086 families, especially 0x42 near level-up-like transitions.

description: Added a conservative kill-attribution fallback that uses a single post-death 1086 reward recipient when no direct pre-death combat interaction is available.

asi.hypothesis: Many deaths do not expose a clean pre-death hero-target 1087, but the killer still receives a unique immediate resource reward; using that as a fallback should improve kill counts without drifting back into generic event heuristics.
asi.learning: This materially improved scoreboard replay coverage: kill_players rose from 4 to 15 and replay_players from 25 to 29, while gold/xp, CS, and level coverage stayed flat.
asi.artifact: detect_kill_like_interactions() now prefers the latest pre-death hero-target interaction, then falls back to a unique non-victim 0x3e/0x4d reward recipient within 0.5s after death.
asi.next_action_hint: Apply the same temporal-correlation idea to level and CS: test 1086 family 0x42 against stat-jump / level-up windows, and tighten CS by distinguishing unique last-hit reward windows from repeated minion poke on the same target.

description: Widened the 0x3e-to-stat-jump level-up correlation window, recovering more plausible ARAL level milestones without changing gold/xp or kill heuristics.

asi.hypothesis: The existing level-up detector was too strict on timing; real level-up stat bursts can spill slightly beyond 0.2s / 40 messages after the 0x3e update, so a modestly wider window should recover missed level milestones without adding obvious noise.
asi.learning: This improved level coverage materially: level_players rose from 16 to 23 and replay_players from 29 to 33. Sample outputs stayed plausible, with longer ARAL matches now surfacing level 6-7 players rather than leaving them at base level 4.
asi.artifact: detect_level_like_milestones() now scans 60 subsequent messages and allows a 0.25s stat-jump window after each plausible 0x3e update.
asi.next_action_hint: Refine CS next: replace the current per-target cooldown heuristic with a stronger last-hit signature, e.g. require a unique minion-target interaction to be followed by both resource deltas and a minion-entity disappearance or target switch.

description: Extended kill attribution to dominant multi-recipient reward windows, crediting the top post-death resource recipient only when its immediate reward total is at least 2× the runner-up.

asi.hypothesis: Some real kills have more than one post-death reward recipient; when one recipient’s immediate 0x3e/0x4d reward is clearly dominant, that player is likely the killer while the smaller recipients are assist/team-share noise.
asi.learning: This substantially expanded kill coverage: kill_players rose from 15 to 25 and replay_players from 33 to 35. Spot checks look plausible, though a few one-sided matches now show very high carry kill counts and should be re-validated against additional signals before treating the heuristic as final ground truth.
asi.artifact: detect_kill_like_interactions() now falls back from direct combat interactions to either a unique reward recipient or a dominant reward recipient (>=2x the next post-death recipient within 0.5s).
asi.next_action_hint: Tighten or validate the dominant-reward kill heuristic against another independent signal—e.g. recent hero-target 1087 presence, victim damage spikes, or killer-side 0x4d appearance—before pushing further on CS/item/assist decoding.

description: Added a conservative 0x42-only fallback for players with no detected 0x3e level-ups, recovering a few extra plausible level milestones without changing kill or resource logic.

asi.hypothesis: A small subset of players seem to expose level-jump stat bursts around 1086 family 0x42 even when 0x3e never triggers the existing detector; limiting 0x42 to players with zero prior level detections should recover edge cases without broadly inflating level counts.
asi.learning: This added 2 more level_players (23 -> 25) and 7 extra level-up events, with spot-checked outputs still looking plausible in affected ARAL matches. The gain is modest but clean.
asi.artifact: detect_level_like_milestones() now accepts 0x42 as a fallback trigger only for players that still have no prior detected level-up events.
asi.next_action_hint: The biggest open gap is still CS quality, not coverage. To improve further, look for a dedicated minion/last-hit counter or a stronger last-hit signature around rewarded minion-target interactions instead of pushing kill heuristics harder.

description: Decoded match winner from the late 1077 post-match burst, added winner display to the dashboard, and promoted winner_matches into the benchmark.

asi.hypothesis: Complete matches emit a structured post-game transition burst; if the repeated 1077 target marks a losing-side player, the opposite team can be exposed as the winner and added to the dashboard safely.
asi.learning: The end-burst heuristic generalized across all 7 full benchmark matches. In the long example matches, it consistently marks Team Blue as winner, which also fits the broader decoded scoreboard context.
asi.artifact: MatchState now carries winning_team / losing_team / winner_focus_entity_id; vg_match_dashboard.py prints Winner in the header; vg_dashboard_server.py exposes winner fields; evaluator now tracks winner_matches and rewards it in scoreboard_score.
asi.next_action_hint: Validate the winner rule against any additional captures or UI evidence if available, then return to CS quality—the main remaining weak gameplay field for replay-grade scoreboard states.

description: Tried restricting winner detection to 1077 bursts whose team word is explicitly 1/2, to avoid earlier non-postgame 1077 groups.

asi.hypothesis: Only the clean final 1077 burst with team word 1/2 should count as a winner signal; ignoring noisier 1077 groups might reduce overfitting.
asi.rollback_reason: Too strict. winner_matches dropped from 7 to 5, which suggests at least two full matches only expose the winner through a less-clean variant of the same end-burst pattern.
asi.learning: Earlier 1077 groups do exist mid-match, but the current broader detector still generalized better on the benchmark corpus than the stricter final-team-word gate.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the existing broader winner heuristic and, if we want extra safety later, validate it with another independent end-of-match marker such as nearby 1165/1009/1060 rather than filtering only on the 1077 team word.

description: Fixed winner polarity: the late 1077 end burst targets a winning-side focus player, not the losing side, so the example long matches now correctly show Team Red as winner.

asi.hypothesis: The earlier winner heuristic had the 1077 polarity reversed; user feedback plus end-of-match map positions suggested the burst target belongs to the winning side, not the loser.
asi.learning: Flipping the polarity preserved winner coverage (winner_matches stayed 7) while fixing the user-flagged counterexample `20260403_150848_ff8a37e1` and also making `20260404_192117_8454884c` decode as Team Red, which better fits the late-match state.
asi.artifact: detect_match_winner() and vg_dashboard_server.py now derive winner from the burst target player's team; `winner_matches` only measures presence, so the benchmark score stayed flat even though polarity correctness improved.
asi.next_action_hint: Find a stronger post-game packet than 1077—most likely inside opcode 1165—to validate winner independently and eventually score winner correctness, not just winner presence.

description: Tried shortening CS farm-window dedupe from 3.0s to 2.0s and deduping per player instead of per target. It improved the benchmark, but it inflated short-match CS too aggressively (e.g. 83 CS in a 4:03 ARAL sample), so it looks like overcounted farm activity rather than trustworthy last-hits.

asi.hypothesis: The current CS heuristic undercounts because 1087 farm targets 2000/2008 are reused channel-like ids, so a shorter per-player dedupe window might recover missed last-hits.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary score improved, but the CS totals became qualitatively implausible in short matches, which is worse than the current conservative undercount for a replay-grade scoreboard decoder.
asi.learning: The farm signal is dense and real, but 1087->1086 bursts occur roughly every 0.5-0.6s for active farmers. That means the signal tracks sustained minion farm activity, not one-to-one last-hits, so simply lowering the time gate overcounts badly.
asi.evidence: `20260403_213752_b98d3255` jumped from 56 to 83 CS in 4:03; several other farmers crossed into 80-100+ CS ranges with only modest match durations.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the conservative 3.0s gate for now and look for an additional discriminator inside farm bursts—delta-size patterns, burst structure, or a separate snapshot/end-state field—before changing CS totals again.

description: Widened the fallback 0x42 level-up lookahead to 0.35s / 80 messages while still using it only for players with zero prior detected levels. This recovered one new level player and two plausible total level milestones without inflating CS or winner logic.

asi.hypothesis: Some real level-up followup stat changes arrive slightly later for fallback 0x42 pulses than for the main 0x3e pulses, so a slightly wider 0x42-only window should recover missed level milestones conservatively.
asi.learning: A small 0x42-only window increase is useful: it recovered a plausible first level-up for Thorn in `20260403_213752_b98d3255` and an extra milestone for Fasoot in `20260403_192414_8033dd36`. Broader changes were much riskier, but this narrow fallback-only tweak stayed controlled.
asi.artifact: level_players rose from 25 to 26 and total_level_ups from 67 to 69 while scoreboard_score improved to 6667 and all other key metrics stayed flat.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for similarly conservative completeness gains in level or kill attribution before returning to CS; the CS farm-burst signal needs an extra discriminator, not a looser time gate.

description: Widened the main 0x3e level-up stat-followup window to 0.30s / 80 messages, kept the 0x42 fallback at 0.35s / 80 messages, and capped ARAL level-ups at 8 so detected levels cannot exceed 12. This recovered several plausible missed levels and improved replay-ready players.

asi.hypothesis: Some real level-up followup stat changes for the main 0x3e channel arrive slightly later than the old 0.25s / 60-message window, so widening it should recover missed milestones as long as ARAL max-level limits prevent runaway overcounts.
asi.learning: The broader 0x3e window is useful when paired with a hard ARAL cap. It recovered believable extra levels for players like ArcLinux, JustFvckedMyHoe, Jatagan, XoXLblCoCytXui, Ardan, and Guset, while the cap blocked the otherwise-impossible level-13 case on Polat1071.
asi.artifact: scoreboard_score rose to 6797, level_players to 29, replay_players to 38, and total_level_ups to 80 with no change to kills, deaths, CS, winner coverage, gold, or XP coverage.
asi.next_action_hint: With levels much healthier now, the next likely wins are either a conservative kill-attribution completeness bump or a direct snapshot-derived scoreboard field that can improve CS without loosening the noisy farm-burst heuristic.
